zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Lucy是Sir William de Lucy的女兒，於1446年出生在英國米德萊可霍爾波恩。她於1465年與William Nanseglos在美國堪薩斯州薩姆納倫敦結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Lucy是Sir William de Lucy的女儿，于1446年出生在英国米德萊可霍爾波恩。她于1465年与William Nanseglos在美国堪萨斯州薩姆納倫敦结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lucy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish and English: variant of   Lucey  .
French: habitational name from any of various places called Lucy, originally named with the Latin personal name Lucius + the locative suffix -acum.

variants.name: Lucey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Maeve, Niall.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Luasaigh, an altered form of Mac Cluasaigh, a Cork name meaning ‘son of Cluasach’, a byname originally denoting someone with large or otherwise noticeable ears (from cluas ‘ear’).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Lucy was born in 1446, in Holborn, Middlesex, England as the daughter of Sir William de Lucy. She married William Nanseglos in 1465, in London, Sumner, Kansas, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Lucy est née en 1446, à Holborn, Middlesex, Angleterre, fille de Sir William de Lucy. Elle a épousé William Nanseglos en 1465, à London, Sumner, Kansas, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Lucy wurde 1446 in Holborn, Middlesex, England, als Tochter von Sir William de Lucy geboren. Sie hat 1465 in London, Sumner, Kansas, Vereinigte Staaten William Nanseglos geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Lucy è nata il nel 1446 nel Holborn, Middlesex, England come figlia di Sir William de Lucy. Lei sposato William Nanseglos nel 1465, nel London, Sumner, Kansas, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Lucyは1446年にイギリスミッドレセックスホールボルンでSir William de Lucyの娘として生まれた。1465年にアメリカ合衆国カンザス州スムナーロンドンでWilliam Nanseglosと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Lucy(은)는 1446년 영국 미들레세스 홀본에서 Sir William de Lucy의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1465년 미국 캔자스 주 섬너 런던에서 William Nanseglos(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Lucy родилась в 1446 г. в местности холборн, миддлесекс, Англия. Её отца звали Sir William de Lucy. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Nanseglos в 1465 г. в местности лондон, сумнир, Канзас, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в местности Англия, Великобритания.
